derivation: 16-bit ripple-carry adder: LSB has no carry-in so 1 half-adder; bits 1-15 each need a carry-in so 15 full-adders.
A demux routes a single input to one of many outputs, and that same operation implements serial-to-parallel conversion, so both (a) and (b) hold; (D) describes a multiplexer.
Pure ALOHA has continuous time: a station transmits whenever it has a frame. Slotted ALOHA forces waiting for the next slot boundary.
ARP resolves internet-layer (IP) addresses to link-layer (MAC) addresses; A=DHCP, C=DNS, D=FTP.

FTP carries control on a separate connection (port 21) from data (port 20), the textbook example of out-of-band control; TCP urgent data travels in the same connection, so it is not out-of-band control.
At most one matching record on each side of the relationship is by definition one-to-one.
Activity, use case, collaboration and sequence diagrams all model behavior over time, so they are UML behavioral diagrams.

A collaboration (communication) diagram shows object interactions as a numbered series of messages over links; sequence diagrams emphasize time ordering and are not offered by name here (only 'object lifeline diagrams').
Deployment diagrams map artifacts (files, libraries, tables) onto the hardware topology of nodes.
Interaction diagrams are the general UML category for dynamic aspects; sequence is only one instance of that category, so the general term is the intended answer.
Class, component, object and deployment diagrams describe static structure, so they are structural diagrams.
In UML, a weak entity dependent on an owner is modeled as a qualified aggregation (the qualifier plays the role of the partial key).
The whole-part (object to its component parts) relationship in UML is aggregation.
A hash table implements an associative array: it hashes a key to a bucket holding the value, so it maps keys to values.
Two or more keys hashing into the same bucket is a collision.
